entries.help: Disable RHI Resources flush which introduces RenderThread stall until all tasks submitted to RHI thread will be completed. Experimental.
entries.name: r.SceneCapture.Experimental.DisableRHIResourcesFlush

entries.help: Number of X tiles to render. Ignored in Perspective mode, works only in Orthographic mode and when r.SceneCapture.OverrideOrthographicTilingValues is on.
entries.name: r.SceneCapture.OrthographicNumXTiles

entries.help: Number of Y tiles to render. Ignored in Perspective mode, works only in Orthographic mode and when r.SceneCapture.OverrideOrthographicTilingValues is on.
entries.name: r.SceneCapture.OrthographicNumYTiles

entries.help: Override defined orthographic values from SceneCaptureComponent2D - Ignored in Perspective mode.
entries.name: r.SceneCapture.OverrideOrthographicTilingValues

entries.help: Defines the memory layout (RGBA) used for the scene color
entries.name: r.SceneColorFormat

entries.help: Allows to clamp the postprocess setting (in percent, Scene chromatic aberration / color fringe to simulate an artifact that happens in real-world lens, mostly visible in the image corners)
entries.name: r.SceneColorFringe.Max

entries.help: 0: off but best for performance
entries.name: r.SceneColorFringeQuality

entries.help: Selects whether HZB for scene depth buffer should be built with async compute.
entries.name: r.SceneDepthHZBAsyncCompute

entries.help: Controls when to perform clean up of the scene renderer.
entries.name: r.SceneRenderCleanUpMode

entries.help: Control the scene render target resize method:
entries.name: r.SceneRenderTargetResizeMethod

entries.help: Forces 'r.SceneRenderTargetResizeMethod' to be respected on all configurations.
entries.name: r.SceneRenderTargetResizeMethodForceOverride

entries.help: Controls the absolute maximum number of rendered pixel before any upscaling such that doesn't go higher than the specified 16:9 resolution of this variable. For instance set this value to 1440 so that you are not rendering more than 2560x1440 = 3.6M pixels. This is useful to set this on PC in your project's DefaultEditor.ini so you are not rendering more pixel on PC in PIE that you would in average on console with your project specific dynamic resolution settings.
entries.name: r.ScreenPercentage.MaxResolution

entries.help: Controls the absolute minimum number of rendered pixel.
entries.name: r.ScreenPercentage.MinResolution

entries.help: Selects mode to control the screen percentage.
entries.name: r.ScreenPercentage.Mode

entries.help: ScreenshotDelegates prevent processing of incoming screenshot request and break some features. This allows to disable them.
entries.name: r.ScreenshotDelegate

entries.help: Override secondary screen percentage for game viewport.
entries.name: r.SecondaryScreenPercentage.GameViewport

entries.help: Enables shaders to only export to relevant rendertargets.
entries.name: r.SelectiveBasePassOutputs

entries.help: Allows to disable the separate translucency feature (all translucency is rendered in separate RT and composited
entries.name: r.SeparateTranslucency

entries.help: Render separate translucency at this percentage of the full resolution.
entries.name: r.SeparateTranslucencyScreenPercentage

entries.help: Upsample method to use on separate translucency.  These are only used when r.SeparateTranslucencyScreenPercentage is less than 100.
entries.name: r.SeparateTranslucencyUpsampleMode

entries.help: Set a target frame rate for the frame pacer.To set 30fps: "r.SetFramePace 30"
entries.name: r.SetFramePace

entries.help: Set the near clipping plane (in cm)
entries.name: r.SetNearClipPlane

entries.help: Set the display resolution for the current game view. Has no effect in the editor.
entries.name: r.SetRes

entries.help: Max (uncompressed) size of a group of shaders to be compressed/decompressed together.If a group exceeds it, it will be evenly split into subgroups which strive to not exceed it. However, if a shader group is down to one shader and still exceeds the limit, the limit will be ignored.
entries.name: r.ShaderCodeLibrary.MaxShaderGroupSize

entries.help: If we wait on shader preloads longer than this amount of seconds, we will log it as a warning.
entries.name: r.ShaderCodeLibrary.MaxShaderPreloadWaitTime

entries.help: if > 0, each shader code library has it's own loading cache.
entries.name: r.ShaderCodeLibrary.SeparateLoadingCache

entries.help: If 1, a bitmap with the used shaders (for each shader library chunk) will be saved at the exit. Works in standalone games only.
entries.name: r.ShaderCodeLibrary.VisualizeShaderUsage

entries.help: If 0, only local (spawned by the engine) ShaderCompileWorkers will be used. If 1, SCWs will be distributed using one of several possible backends (XGE, FASTBuild, SN-DBS)
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.AllowDistributedCompilation

entries.help: Minimum interval (in seconds) between printing out debugging stats (by default, no closer than each 3 minutes).
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.CacheStatsPrintoutInterval

entries.help: If set to 1, the shader compiler will crash on hung shadermaps.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.CrashOnHungShaderMaps

entries.help: If true, worker input files will be saved for each individual compile job alongside other debug data (note that r.DumpShaderDebugInfo must also be enabled for this to function)
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDumpWorkerInputs

entries.help: Maximum number of seconds we expect to pass between getting distributed controller complete a task (this is used to detect problems with the distribution controllers).
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DistributedControllerTimeout

entries.help: Minimum number of shaders to compile with a distributed controller.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DistributedMinBatchSize

entries.help: if != 0, unpreprocessed input of the shader compiler jobs will be dumped into the debug directory for closer inspection. This is a debugging feature which is disabled by default.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DumpCompileJobInputs

entries.help: if != 0, DDC keys for each shadermap will be dumped into project's Saved directory (ShaderDDCKeys subdirectory)
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DumpDDCKeys

entries.help: When 1, shader compiler warnings are emitted to the log for all shaders as they are loaded.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.EmitWarningsOnLoad

entries.help: if != 0, shader compiler cache (based on the unpreprocessed input hash) will be disabled. By default, it is enabled.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCache

entries.help: Skips compilation of all shaders on Material and Material Instance PostLoad and relies on on-demand shader compilation to compile what is needed.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCacheDDC

entries.help: Whether to cache shaders in the job cache to your local machine or remotely to the network.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCacheDDCEnableRemotePolicy

entries.help: Maximum number of preprocessed shader sources to dump as a build artifact on shader compile errors. By default 10.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.MaxDumpedShaderSources

entries.help: if != 0, shader compiler cache will be limited to this many megabytes (16GB by default). If 0, the usage will be unlimited. Minimum of this or r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryPercent applies.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryMB

entries.help: if != 0, shader compiler cache will be limited to this percentage of available physical RAM (5% by default). If 0, the usage will be unlimited. Minimum of this or r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryMB applies.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryPercent

entries.help: Maximum number of seconds a single shadermap (which can be comprised of multiple jobs) can be compiled after being considered hung.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.ShadermapCompilationTimeout

entries.help: By default, task files for SCW will be read/written sequentially, but if we ever spend more than this time (0.3s by default) doing that, we'll switch to parallel.We don't default to parallel writes as it increases the CPU overhead from the shader compiler.
entries.name: r.ShaderCompiler.TooLongIOThresholdSeconds

entries.help: 0: Default, 1: Enable various shader development utilities, such as the ability to retry on failed shader compile, and extra logging as shaders are compiled.
entries.name: r.ShaderDevelopmentMode

entries.help: if != 0, shader library will produce extended stats, including the textual representation
entries.name: r.ShaderLibrary.PrintExtendedStats

entries.help: 1 generates the cache every run, 0 generates it only when it is missing.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.AlwaysGenerateOSCache

entries.help: Set the time where any logged PSO's will be saved if the number is < r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveAfterPSOsLogged. Disabled when r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveAfterPSOsLogged is 0
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.AutoSaveTime

entries.help: Set the time where any logged PSO's will be saved when -logpso is on the command line.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.AutoSaveTimeBoundPSO

entries.help: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to compile in a single batch operation when compiling in the background. Defaults to a maximum of 1 per frame, due to async. file IO it is less in practice.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BackgroundBatchSize

entries.help: The target time (in ms) to spend precompiling each frame when in the background or 0.0 to disable. When precompiling is faster the batch size will grow and when slower will shrink to attempt to occupy the full amount. Defaults to 0.0 (off).
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BackgroundBatchTime

entries.help: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to compile in a single batch operation when compiling takes priority. Defaults to a maximum of 50 per frame, due to async. file IO it is less in practice.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BatchSize

entries.help: The target time (in ms) to spend precompiling each frame when compiling takes priority or 0.0 to disable. When precompiling is faster the batch size will grow and when slower will shrink to attempt to occupy the full amount. Defaults to 16.0 (max. ms per-frame of precompilation).
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BatchTime

entries.help: 1 Enables the OS level clear after install, 0 disables it.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.ClearOSCache

entries.help: Close the current pipeline file cache.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.Close

entries.help: Disables precompilation of compute PSOs (replayed from a recorded file) on start. This is a safety switch in case things go wrong
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.DoNotPrecompileComputePSO

entries.help: 1 Enables the PipelineFileCache, 0 disables it.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.Enabled

entries.help: Set non zero to use GameFileMask during PSO precompile - recording should always save out the usage masks to make that data availble when needed.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.GameFileMaskEnabled

entries.help: Only compile PSOs from the GlobalShader cache when runtime PSOPrecaching is enabled (default disabled)
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.GlobalShadersOnlyWhenPSOPrecaching

entries.help: Non-Zero: If we load a PSO cache, then lazy load from the shader code library. This assumes the PSO cache is more or less complete. This will only work on RHIs that support the library+Hash CreateShader API (GRHISupportsLazyShaderCodeLoading == true).
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.LazyLoadShadersWhenPSOCacheIsPresent

entries.help: 1 Logs new PSO entries into the file cache and allows saving.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.LogPSO

entries.help: The maximum time to allow a PSO to be precompiled.  if greather than 0, the amount of wall time we will allow pre-compile of PSOs and then switch to background processing.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.MaxPrecompileTime

entries.help: The minimum bind count to allow a PSO to be precompiled.  Changes to this value will not affect PSOs that have already been removed from consideration.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.MinBindCount

entries.help: Close and reopen the user cache.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.Open

entries.help: Mask used to precompile the cache. Defaults to all PSOs (-1)
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PreCompileMask

entries.help: Set non zero to PreOptimize PSOs - this allows some PSOs to be compiled in the foreground before going in to game
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PreOptimizeEnabled

entries.help: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to compile in a single batch operation when pre-optimizing the cache. Defaults to a maximum of 50 per frame, due to async. file IO it is less in practice.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PrecompileBatchSize

entries.help: The target time (in ms) to spend precompiling each frame when cpre-optimizing or 0.0 to disable. When precompiling is faster the batch size will grow and when slower will shrink to attempt to occupy the full amount. Defaults to 10.0 (off).
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PrecompileBatchTime

entries.help: 1 prints descriptions for all new PSO entries to the log/console while 0 does not. 2 prints additional details about graphics PSO. Defaults to 0.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PrintNewPSODescriptors

entries.help: 1 reports new PSO entries via a delegate, but does not record or modify any cache file.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.ReportPSO

entries.help: Save the current pipeline file cache.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.Save

entries.help: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to log before automatically saving. 0 will disable automatic saving (which is the default now, as automatic saving is found to be broken).
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveAfterPSOsLogged

entries.help: If > 0 then a log of all bound PSOs for this run of the program will be saved to a writable user cache file. Defaults to 0 but is forced on with -logpso.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveBoundPSOLog

entries.help: If > 0 then any missed PSOs will be saved to a writable user cache file for subsequent runs to load and avoid in-game hitches. Enabled by default on macOS only.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveUserCache

entries.help: 1 enables sending telemetry of PSO cache, 0 disables it
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SendSilentEvents

entries.help: Sets the compilation batch mode, which should be one of:
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SetBatchMode

entries.help: The number of new PSOs to reach in order to trigger sending telemetry
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SilentEventsGranularity

entries.help: Sets the startup mode for the PSO cache, determining what the cache does after initialisation:
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.StartupMode

entries.help: The amount of time in days between running the garbage collection on unused PSOs in the user cache. Use a negative value to disable.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.UserCacheUnusedElementCheckPeriod

entries.help: The amount of time in days to keep unused PSO entries in the cache.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.UserCacheUnusedElementRetainDays

entries.help: Enable using Shader pipelines.
entries.name: r.ShaderPipelines

entries.help: ShaderPrint debugging toggle.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint

entries.help: ShaderPrint font size.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.FontSize

entries.help: ShaderPrint horizontal spacing between symbols.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.FontSpacingX

entries.help: ShaderPrint vertical spacing between symbols.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.FontSpacingY

entries.help: Lock the line drawing.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.Lock

entries.help: ShaderPrint output buffer size.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxCharacters

entries.help: ShaderPrint max line count.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxLine

entries.help: ShaderPrint max triangle count.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxTriangle

entries.help: ShaderPrint max widget count.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxWidget

entries.help: Enable zoom magnification around the mouse cursor.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom

entries.help: Select in which corner the zoom magnifer is displayed (0:top-left, 1:top-right, 2:bottom-right, 3:bottom-left).
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom.Corner

entries.help: Zoom factor for magnification around the mouse cursor.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom.Factor

entries.help: Number of pixels magnified around the mouse cursor.
entries.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom.Pixel

entries.help: Allows shader compilation through external ShaderCompileWorker processes.
entries.name: r.Shaders.AllowCompilingThroughWorkers

entries.help: When enabled, this tells supported shader compilers to generate symbols based on source files.
entries.name: r.Shaders.AllowUniqueSymbols

entries.help: Whether to enforce bounds-checking & flush-to-zero/ignore for buffer reads & writes in shaders. Defaults to 1 (enabled). Not all shader languages can omit bounds checking.
entries.name: r.Shaders.BoundsChecking

entries.help: 0 => DO_CHECK=0, DO_GUARD_SLOW=0, 1 => DO_CHECK=1, DO_GUARD_SLOW=0, 2 => DO_CHECK=1, DO_GUARD_SLOW=1 for all shaders.
entries.name: r.Shaders.CheckLevel

entries.help: Oodle compression method for the shader code, from fastest to slowest to decode.
entries.name: r.Shaders.CompressionFormat.Oodle.Algo

entries.help: Oodle compression level. This mostly trades encode speed vs compression ratio, decode speed is determined by r.Shaders.CompressionFormat.Oodle.Algo
entries.name: r.Shaders.CompressionFormat.Oodle.Level

entries.help: Enables generation of extra shader data that can be used at runtime. This includes shader names and other platform specific data.
entries.name: r.Shaders.ExtraData

entries.help: Whether to use fast-math optimisations in shaders.
entries.name: r.Shaders.FastMath

entries.help: Specifies whether the shader compiler should preserve or unroll flow-control in shader code.
entries.name: r.Shaders.FlowControlMode

entries.help: Forces DirectX Shader Compiler (DXC) to be used for all shaders instead of HLSLcc if supported.
entries.name: r.Shaders.ForceDXC

entries.help: Enables generation of data for shader debugging when compiling shaders. This explicitly does not write any shader symbols to disk.
entries.name: r.Shaders.GenerateSymbols

entries.help: Whether to optimize shaders.  When using graphical debuggers like Nsight it can be useful to disable this on startup.
entries.name: r.Shaders.Optimize

entries.help: EXPERIMENTAL: Run a preprocessing step that removes unreferenced code before compiling shaders.
entries.name: r.Shaders.RemoveDeadCode

entries.help: Skips shader compression after compiling. Shader compression time can be quite significant when using debug shaders. This CVar is only valid in non-shipping/test builds.
entries.name: r.Shaders.SkipCompression

entries.help: Override output location of shader symbols. If the path contains the text '{Platform}', that will be replaced with the shader platform string.
entries.name: r.Shaders.SymbolPathOverride

entries.help: Enables debugging of shaders in platform specific graphics debuggers. This will generate and write shader symbols.
entries.name: r.Shaders.Symbols

entries.help: Whether to use the refactored GBuffer that autogenerates encode/decode functions. Will be removed before UE5 ships.
entries.name: r.Shaders.UseGBufferRefactor

entries.help: Executes shader preprocessing via the legacy MCPP preprocessor (instead of the new STB preprocessor).
entries.name: r.Shaders.UseLegacyPreprocessor

entries.help: Enabled shader compiler validation warnings and errors.
entries.name: r.Shaders.Validation

entries.help: Whether to treat warnings as errors when running the shader compiler. Defaults to 0 (disabled). Not all compilers support this mode.
entries.name: r.Shaders.WarningsAsErrors

entries.help: Enables writing shader symbols to disk for platforms that support that. This explicitly does not enable generation of shader symbols.
entries.name: r.Shaders.WriteSymbols

entries.help: 0: Export as loose files.
entries.name: r.Shaders.WriteSymbols.Zip

entries.help: Whether to enforce zero initialise local variables of primitive type in shaders. Defaults to 1 (enabled). Not all shader languages can omit zero initialisation.
entries.name: r.Shaders.ZeroInitialise

entries.help: 0 to disable energy conservation on shading models.
entries.name: r.Shading.EnergyConservation

entries.help: Energy conservation table format 0: 16bits, 1: 32bits.
entries.name: r.Shading.EnergyConservation.Format

entries.help: 0 to disable energy preservation on shading models, i.e. the energy attenuation on diffuse lighting caused by the specular reflection. Require energy conservation to be enabled
entries.name: r.Shading.EnergyPreservation

entries.help: Enable/disable furnace for shading validation.
entries.name: r.Shading.FurnaceTest

entries.help: Number of sampler per pixel used for furnace tests.
entries.name: r.Shading.FurnaceTest.SampleCount

entries.help: If enabled, will always allocate shadow map atlases at the maximum resolution rather than trimming unused space. This will waste more memory but can possibly reduce render target pool fragmentation and thrash.
entries.name: r.Shadow.AlwaysAllocateMaxResolutionAtlases

entries.help: The maximum number of cascades with which to render dynamic directional light shadows.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSM.MaxCascades

entries.help: Allows to scale the cascaded shadow map transition region. Clamped within 0..2.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSM.TransitionScale

entries.help: Bias projection cap for CSM shadows. Can be negative. Large values will accept more distant shadow casters.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMBiasProjectionDistanceCap

entries.help: 0: Render CSM every frame.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMCaching

entries.help: 0: [Vanilla UE] Unbound volume for CSM.1: Cap projection distance for CSM based on Trace Distance and r.Shadow.CSMMaxProjectionDistance.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMCapProjectionDistance

entries.help: Constant depth bias used by CSM
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMDepthBias

entries.help: Whether to use depth bounds tests rather than stencil tests for the CSM bounds
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMDepthBoundsTest

entries.help: Quantization step size in degrees for FOV values used by CSM. This prevents shadow flickering when the main view FOV changes.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMFOVQuantizationStepSize

entries.help: Maximum projection distance for CSM shadows. The minimum of TraceDistance and this is used.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMMaxProjectionDistance

entries.help: Maximum projection distance for CSM shadows. The minimum of TraceDistance and this is used.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMMinProjectionDistance

entries.help: Receiver bias used by CSM. Value between 0 and 1.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMReceiverBias

entries.help: The minimum ratio of the overlap area for CSM scrolling.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMScrollingOverlapAreaThrottle

entries.help: Multiplier for the CSM distance fade
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMShadowDistanceFadeoutMultiplier

entries.help: Slope scale depth bias used by CSM
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMSlopeScaleDepthBias

entries.help: Scale applied to the penumbra size of Cascaded Shadow Map splits, useful for minimizing the transition between splits
entries.name: r.Shadow.CSMSplitPenumbraScale

entries.help: Whether preshadows can be cached as an optimization
entries.name: r.Shadow.CachePreshadow

entries.help: Whether primitives whose materials use World Position Offset should be considered movable for cached shadowmaps.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CacheWPOPrimitives

entries.help: When enabled, movable point and spot light whole scene shadow depths from static primitives will be cached as an optimization.
entries.name: r.Shadow.CacheWholeSceneShadows

entries.help: Whether movable primitives should cast a shadow from cached whole scene shadows (movable point and spot lights).
entries.name: r.Shadow.CachedShadowsCastFromMovablePrimitives

entries.help: Choose the denoising algorithm.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser

entries.help: Number of samples to use to convolve the history over time.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.HistoryConvolutionSamples

entries.help: Maximum number of shadow to denoise at the same time.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.MaxBatchSize

entries.help: Number of pre-convolution passes (default = 1).
entries.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.PreConvolution

entries.help: Maximum number of samples for the reconstruction pass (default = 16).
entries.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.ReconstructionSamples

entries.help: Forces the compilation of the vslayer shader permutation even if the platform (RHI) does not declare compile-time support through RHISupportsVertexShaderLayer.Enabled by default for windows/SM5 as DX11 almost universally supports this at runtime.
entries.name: r.Shadow.DetectVertexShaderLayerAtRuntime

entries.help: visualize preshadow frustums when the shadowfrustums show flag is enabled
entries.name: r.Shadow.DrawPreshadowFrustums

entries.help: Allows modulated shadows to affect the shadow caster. (mobile only)
entries.name: r.Shadow.EnableModulatedSelfShadow

entries.help: Controls the rate at which shadows are faded out
entries.name: r.Shadow.FadeExponent

entries.help: Resolution in texels below which shadows are faded out
entries.name: r.Shadow.FadeResolution

entries.help: Overriding far shadow distance for all directional lighst
entries.name: r.Shadow.FarShadowDistanceOverride

entries.help: Notice: only selected geometry types (static meshes and landscapes) respect this value.
entries.name: r.Shadow.FarShadowStaticMeshLODBias

entries.help: Chooses the shadow filtering method.
entries.name: r.Shadow.FilterMethod

entries.help: Force Serial shadow passes to render in 1 pass.
entries.name: r.Shadow.ForceSerialSingleRenderPass

entries.help: Whether to force all components to act as if they have bSingleSampleShadowFromStationaryLights enabled.  Useful for scalability when dynamic shadows are disabled.
entries.name: r.Shadow.ForceSingleSampleShadowingFromStationary

entries.help: Enables culling of shadow casters that do not intersect the convex hull of the light origin and view frustum.
entries.name: r.Shadow.LightViewConvexHullCull

entries.help: Max square dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering Cascaded Shadow depths. Range 4 to hardware limit. Higher = better quality shadows but at a performance cost.
entries.name: r.Shadow.MaxCSMResolution

entries.help: The maximum number of extra static shadow subjects need to be drawed when scrolling CSM.
entries.name: r.Shadow.MaxCSMScrollingStaticShadowSubjects

entries.help: Max number of far shadow cascades that can be cast from a directional light
entries.name: r.Shadow.MaxNumFarShadowCascades

entries.help: Maximum number of point light shadow cache updates allowed per frame.Only affect updates caused by resolution change. -1 means no limit.
entries.name: r.Shadow.MaxNumPointShadowCacheUpdatesPerFrame

entries.help: Maximum number of spot light shadow cache updates allowed per frame.Only affect updates caused by resolution change. -1 means no limit.
entries.name: r.Shadow.MaxNumSpotShadowCacheUpdatesPerFrame

entries.help: Max square dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering shadow depths. Range 4 to hardware limit. Higher = better quality shadows but at a performance cost.
entries.name: r.Shadow.MaxResolution

entries.help: Mazimum size of the softening kernels in pixels.
entries.name: r.Shadow.MaxSoftKernelSize

entries.help: Minimum dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering preshadow depths
entries.name: r.Shadow.MinPreShadowResolution

entries.help: Minimum dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering shadow subject depths
entries.name: r.Shadow.MinResolution

entries.help: Enables shadows from Nanite meshes.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Nanite

entries.help: LOD bias for nanite geometry in shadows. 0 = full detail. >0 = reduced detail.
entries.name: r.Shadow.NaniteLODBias

entries.help: Produce Nanite geometry streaming requests from shadow map rendering.
entries.name: r.Shadow.NaniteUpdateStreaming

entries.help: Enables HZB for Nanite shadows.
entries.name: r.Shadow.NaniteUseHZB

entries.help: Whether to use occlusion culling on cascaded shadow maps.  Disabled by default because rapid view changes reveal new regions too quickly for latent occlusion queries to work with.
entries.name: r.Shadow.OcclusionCullCascadedShadowMaps

entries.help: Override distant cascade start index for culling purposes
entries.name: r.Shadow.Optimization.DistantCascadeStartIndexOverride

entries.help: Whether to render per object shadows (character casting on world)
entries.name: r.Shadow.PerObject

entries.help: Minimum cast distance for Per-Object shadows, i.e., CastDistDance = Max(r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceRadiusScale * object-radius, r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceMin).
entries.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceMin

entries.help: PerObjectCastDistanceRadiusScale The scale factor multiplied with the radius of the object to calculate the maximum distance a per-object directional shadow can reach. This will only take effect after a certain (large) radius. Default is 8 times the object radius.
entries.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceRadiusScale

entries.help: Constant depth bias used by per-object shadows from directional lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectDirectionalDepthBias

entries.help: Slope scale depth bias used by per-object shadows from directional lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectDirectionalSlopeDepthBias

entries.help: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for per-object projected shadows from spot lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectSpotLightDepthBias

entries.help: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for per-object projected shadows from spot lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectSpotLightSlopeDepthBias

entries.help: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from point lights. (0.03 avoids peter paning but has some shadow acne)
entries.name: r.Shadow.PointLightDepthBias

entries.help: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from point lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.PointLightSlopeScaleDepthBias

entries.help: Resolution in texels below which preshadows are faded out
entries.name: r.Shadow.PreShadowFadeResolution

entries.help: How much bounds will be expanded when rendering a cached preshadow (0.15 = 15% larger)
entries.name: r.Shadow.PreshadowExpand

entries.help: Whether to allow preshadows (static world casting on character)
entries.name: r.Shadow.Preshadows

entries.help: When enabled, static meshes render their lowest detail level into preshadow depth maps.  Disabled by default as it causes artifacts with poor quality LODs (tree billboard).
entries.name: r.Shadow.PreshadowsForceLowestDetailLevel

entries.help: Cull shadow casters if they are too small, value is the minimal screen space bounding sphere radius
entries.name: r.Shadow.RadiusThreshold

entries.help: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from rect lights. (0.03 avoids peter paning but has some shadow acne)
entries.name: r.Shadow.RectLightDepthBias

entries.help: Receiver bias used by rect light. Value between 0 and 1.
entries.name: r.Shadow.RectLightReceiverBias

entries.help: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from rect lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.RectLightSlopeScaleDepthBias

entries.help: DEPRECATED: If 1 (default) then setting Shadow Resolution Scale to zero disables shadow maps for the light.
entries.name: r.Shadow.ResolutionScaleZeroDisablesSm

entries.help: Max Slope depth bias used for shadows for all lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.ShadowMaxSlopeScaleDepthBias

entries.help: When enabled, CPU culling will ignore nanite meshes.
entries.name: r.Shadow.SkipCullingNaniteMeshes

entries.help: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for whole-scene projected shadows from spot lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightDepthBias

entries.help: Receiver bias used by spotlights. Value between 0 and 1.
entries.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightReceiverBias

entries.help: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for whole-scene projected shadows from spot lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightSlopeDepthBias

entries.help: Transition scale for spotlights
entries.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightTransitionScale

entries.help: Whether to use stencil light culling during shadow projection (default) or only depth.
entries.name: r.Shadow.StencilCulling

entries.help: Removes stencil clears between shadow projections by zeroing the stencil during testing
entries.name: r.Shadow.StencilOptimization

entries.help: The ratio of subject pixels to shadow texels for point lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.TexelsPerPixelPointlight

entries.help: The ratio of subject pixels to shadow texels for rect lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.TexelsPerPixelRectlight

entries.help: The ratio of subject pixels to shadow texels for spotlights
entries.name: r.Shadow.TexelsPerPixelSpotlight

entries.help: This controls the 'fade in' region between a caster and where its shadow shows up.  Larger values make a smaller region which will have more self shadowing artifacts
entries.name: r.Shadow.TransitionScale

entries.help: Enable/Disable translucency shadows on a per-project basis. Turning off can significantly reduce the number of permutations if your project has many translucent materials.
entries.name: r.Shadow.TranslucentPerObject.ProjectEnabled

entries.help: DynamicShadowCascades to use when using CSM to preview unbuilt lighting from a directional light
entries.name: r.Shadow.UnbuiltNumWholeSceneDynamicShadowCascades

entries.help: Whether to render unbuilt preview shadows in game.  When enabled and lighting is not built, expensive preview shadows will be rendered in game.  When disabled, lighting in game and editor won't match which can appear to be a bug.
entries.name: r.Shadow.UnbuiltPreviewInGame

entries.help: WholeSceneDynamicShadowRadius to use when using CSM to preview unbuilt lighting from a directional light
entries.name: r.Shadow.UnbuiltWholeSceneDynamicShadowRadius

entries.help: Whether to use the primitive octree for shadow subject culling.  The octree culls large groups of primitives at a time, but introduces cache misses walking the data structure.
entries.name: r.Shadow.UseOctreeForCulling

entries.help: AccumulateStats
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.AccumulateStats

entries.help: Turn on to enable caching
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache

entries.help: Enable support for panning cached clipmap pages for directional lights.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.ClipmapPanning

entries.help: If enabled, Primitive Proxies that are marked as having deformable meshes (HasDeformableMesh() == true) causes invalidations regardless of whether their transforms are updated.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.DeformableMeshesInvalidate

entries.help: Turn on debug render cache invalidating instance bounds, heat mapped by number of pages invalidated.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.DrawInvalidatingBounds

entries.help: Forces the clipmap to always invalidate, useful to emulate a moving sun to avoid misrepresenting cache performance.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.ForceInvalidateDirectional

entries.help: Enables testing HZB for Virtual Shadow Map invalidations.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.InvalidateUseHZB

entries.help: Beyond this distance in world units, material position effects (e.g., WPO or PDO) cease to cause VSM invalidations.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.MaxMaterialPositionInvalidationRange

entries.help: When enabled, caches static objects in separate pages from dynamic objects.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.StaticSeparate

entries.help: First level of the clipmap to mark coarse pages for. Lower values allow higher resolution coarse pages near the camera but increase total page counts.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.FirstCoarseLevel

entries.help: First level of the virtual clipmap. Lower values allow higher resolution shadows closer to the camera.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.FirstLevel

entries.help: Last level of the clipmap to mark coarse pages for. Higher values provide dense clipmap data for a longer radius but increase total page counts.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.LastCoarseLevel

entries.help: Last level of the virtual climap. Indirectly determines radius the clipmap can cover.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.LastLevel

entries.help: If greater than zero, clamps the camera viewport dimensions used to adjust the clipmap resolution.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.MinCameraViewportWidth

entries.help: Conservative culling removes the frustum-clipped culling volume for the non-nanite geometry for VSM rendering. This means a lot more geometry is submitted, and also marked as rendered.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.UseConservativeCulling

entries.help: Scale of the clipmap level depth range relative to the radius. Should generally be at least 10 or it will result in excessive cache invalidations.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.ZRangeScale

entries.help: If a dynamic (non-nanite) instance has a smaller footprint, it should not be drawn into a coarse page.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CoarsePagePixelThresholdDynamic

entries.help: If a dynamic Nanite instance has a smaller footprint, it should not be drawn into a coarse page.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CoarsePagePixelThresholdDynamicNanite

entries.help: If a static (non-nanite) instance has a smaller footprint, it should not be drawn into a coarse page.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CoarsePagePixelThresholdStatic

entries.help: When enabled does not generate shadow data for pixels that are backfacing to the light.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CullBackfacingPixels

entries.help: Fraction of footprint size below which start force-caching lights that are invalidated (i.e., are moving or re-added)
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.DistantLightForceCacheFootprintFraction

entries.help: Control whether distant light mode is enabled for local lights.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.DistantLightMode

entries.help: Enable Virtual Shadow Maps.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Enable

entries.help: Forces full HZB update every frame rather than just dirty pages.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ForceFullHZBUpdate

entries.help: If enabled, disallow creation of conventional non-virtual shadow maps for any lights that get a virtual shadow map.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ForceOnlyVirtualShadowMaps

entries.help: Marks coarse pages in directional light virtual shadow maps so that low resolution data is available everywhere.Ability to disable is primarily for profiling and debugging.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MarkCoarsePagesDirectional

entries.help: Marks coarse pages in local light virtual shadow maps so that low resolution data is available everywhere.Ability to disable is primarily for profiling and debugging.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MarkCoarsePagesLocal

entries.help: Marks pages in virtual shadow maps based on depth buffer pixels. Ability to disable is primarily for profiling and debugging.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MarkPixelPages

entries.help: Maximum number of distant lights to update each frame.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MaxDistantUpdatePerFrame

entries.help: Maximum number of physical pages in the pool.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MaxPhysicalPages

entries.help: Maximum number of physical pages in the pool for each scene capture component.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MaxPhysicalPagesSceneCapture

entries.help: Enable Nanite CPU-side visibility filtering of draw commands, depends on r.Nanite.MaterialVisibility being enabled.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Nanite.MaterialVisibility

entries.help: Include non-Nanite geometry in coarse pages.Rendering non-Nanite geometry into large coarse pages can be expensive; disabling this can be a significant performance win.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.IncludeInCoarsePages

entries.help: Cull Non-Nanite instances using HZB. If set to 2, attempt to use Nanite-HZB from the current frame.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.UseHZB

entries.help: Enable support for non-nanite Virtual Shadow Maps.Read-only and to be set in a config file (requires restart).
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNaniteVSM

entries.help: Receiver offset along surface normal for shadow lookup. Scaled by distance to camera.Higher values avoid artifacts on surfaces nearly parallel to the light, but also visibility offset shadows and increase the chance of hitting unmapped pages.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NormalBias

entries.help: Projects all local light virtual shadow maps in a single pass for better performance.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.OnePassProjection

entries.help: Maximum lights per pixel that get full filtering when using one pass projection and clustered shading.Generally set to 8 (32bpp), 16 (64bpp) or 32 (128bpp). Lower values require less transient VRAM during the lighting pass.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.OnePassProjection.MaxLightsPerPixel

entries.help: Allows skipping the screen space shadow mask entirely when only a virtual shadow map would write into it.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.OnePassProjection.SkipScreenShadowMask

entries.help: If a screen pixel falls within this fraction of a page border for directional lights, the adacent page will also be mapped.Higher values can reduce page misses at screen edges or disocclusions, but increase total page counts.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.PageDilationBorderSizeDirectional

entries.help: If a screen pixel falls within this fraction of a page border for local lights, the adacent page will also be mapped.Higher values can reduce page misses at screen edges or disocclusions, but increase total page counts.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.PageDilationBorderSizeLocal

entries.help: Bias applied to LOD calculations for local lights. -1.0 doubles resolution, 1.0 halves it and so on.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ResolutionLodBiasLocal

entries.help: Shoot fewer rays in fully shadowed and unshadowed regions. Currently only supported with OnePassProjection.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.AdaptiveRayCount

entries.help: Maximum depth slope when extrapolating behind occluders for directional lights.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.ExtrapolateMaxSlopeDirectional

entries.help: Maximum depth slope when extrapolating behind occluders for local lights.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.ExtrapolateMaxSlopeLocal

entries.help: Max angle (in radians) a ray is allowed to span from the light's perspective for local lights.Smaller angles limit the screen space size of shadow penumbra. Larger angles lead to more noise.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.MaxRayAngleFromLight

entries.help: Maximum depth slope. Low values produce artifacts if shadow resolution is insufficient. High values can worsen light leaks near occluders and sparkly pixels in shadowed areas.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.MaxSlopeBiasLocal

entries.help: Ray count for shadow map tracing of directional lights. 0 = disabled.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.RayCountDirectional

entries.help: Ray count for shadow map tracing of local lights. 0 = disabled.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.RayCountLocal

entries.help: Length of ray to shoot for directional lights, scaled by distance to camera.Shorter rays limit the screen space size of shadow penumbra. Longer rays require more samples to avoid shadows disconnecting from contact points.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.RayLengthScaleDirectional

entries.help: Shadow map samples per ray for directional lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.SamplesPerRayDirectional

entries.help: Shadow map samples per ray for local lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.SamplesPerRayLocal

entries.help: Applies a dither to the shadow map ray casts for directional lights to help hide aliasing due to insufficient shadow resolution.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.TexelDitherScaleDirectional

entries.help: Applies a dither to the shadow map ray casts for local lights to help hide aliasing due to insufficient shadow resolution.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.TexelDitherScaleLocal

entries.help: Length of the screen space shadow trace (smart shadow bias) before the virtual shadow map lookup.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ScreenRayLength

entries.help: Set to the number of clipmap you want to show stats for (-1 == off)
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ShowClipmapStats

entries.help: Enable Virtual Shadow Maps per-light draw events - may affect performance especially when there are many small lights in the scene.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ShowLightDrawEvents

entries.help: ShowStats, also toggle shaderprint one!
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ShowStats

entries.help: Minimum source angle (in degrees) used for shadow & transmittance of sub-surface materials with directional lights
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SubsurfaceShadowMinSourceAngle

entries.help: Quality of shadow for lit translucent surfaces. This will be applied on all translucent surfaces, and has high-performance impact.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.TranslucentQuality

entries.help: Switch between implementing the far shadow culling logic for VSMs.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.UseFarShadowCulling

entries.help: Enables HZB for (Nanite) Virtual Shadow Maps - Non-Nanite unfortunately has a separate flag with different semantics: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.UseHZB.
entries.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.UseHZB

entries.help: Amount of memory that can be spent caching whole scene shadows.  ShadowMap allocations in a single frame can cause this to be exceeded.
entries.name: r.Shadow.WholeSceneShadowCacheMb

entries.help: How many unbuilt light-primitive interactions there can be for a light before the light switches to whole scene shadows
entries.name: r.Shadow.WholeSceneShadowUnbuiltInteractionThreshold

entries.help: Defines the shadow method which allows to adjust for quality or performance.
entries.name: r.ShadowQuality

entries.help: Used for 0 Cascade for CSM or for other shadow types (Point lights, Spot lights), but still ignored for VSM
entries.name: r.Shadows.CSM.MinBoundsRadius.Cascade0

entries.help: Enable culling too small objects for shadow maps
entries.name: r.Shadows.CSM.MinBoundsRadius.Enabled

entries.help: If enabled, shadows will use the shared external access queue, minimizing unnecessary transitions
entries.name: r.ShadowsUseSharedExternalAccessQueue

entries.help: If not zero, draw all precomputed visibility cells.
entries.name: r.ShowPrecomputedVisibilityCells

entries.help: If not zero, draw relevant precomputed visibility cells only.
entries.name: r.ShowRelevantPrecomputedVisibilityCells

entries.help: When set to 1, will display all warnings.
entries.name: r.ShowShaderCompilerWarnings

entries.help: If non-zero, mobile setting for MinLOD will be stored in the cooked data for desktop platforms
entries.name: r.SkeletalMesh.KeepMobileMinLODSettingOnDesktop

entries.help: Whether a material needs to be referenced by at least one unstripped mesh LOD to be considered as used.
entries.name: r.SkeletalMesh.LODMaterialReference

entries.help: If set will strip skeletal mesh LODs under the minimum renderable LOD for the target platform during cooking.
entries.name: r.SkeletalMesh.StripMinLodDataDuringCooking

entries.help: Enable the use of the cloth blend weight value set by the skeletal mesh component. When disabled all cloth blend weight will become 0.
entries.name: r.SkeletalMeshClothBlend.Enabled

entries.help: LOD bias for skeletal meshes (does not affect animation editor viewports).
entries.name: r.SkeletalMeshLODBias

entries.help: Scale factor for the screen radius used in computing discrete LOD for skeletal meshes. (0.25-1)
entries.name: r.SkeletalMeshLODRadiusScale

entries.help: Name of what skeletal mesh reduction module to choose. If blank it chooses any that exist.
entries.name: r.SkeletalMeshReductionModule

entries.help: Allow occlusion queries fo skeletal meshes with CustomDepth flag enabled. Default value is 0. Set to 1 can break some passes like X-ray vision or vision through the walls because occluded skeletal meshes won't be rendered in custom depth.
entries.name: r.SkeletalMeshes.EnableOcclusionForCustomDepthPrimitives

entries.help: Whether or not to allow the GPU skin Cache system to be enabled.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.Allow

entries.help: 0: off (default)
entries.name: r.SkinCache.AllowDupedVertsForRecomputeTangents

entries.help: Trigger a render capture for the next skin cache dispatches.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.Capture

entries.help: Whether or not to compile the GPU compute skinning cache shaders.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.CompileShaders

entries.help: A scaling constant passed to the SkinCache shader, useful for debugging
entries.name: r.SkinCache.Debug

entries.help: Default behavior if all skeletal meshes are included/excluded from the skin cache. If Support Ray Tracing is enabled on a mesh, will force inclusive behavior on that mesh.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.DefaultBehavior

entries.help: Maximum number of compute shader dispatches which are batched together into a single command list to fix potential TDRs.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.MaxDispatchesPerCmdList

entries.help: Maximum amount of primitives which are batched together into a single command list to fix potential TDRs.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.MaxRayTracingPrimitivesPerCmdList

entries.help: Whether or not to use the GPU compute skinning cache.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.Mode

entries.help: How many intermediate buffers to use for intermediate results while
entries.name: r.SkinCache.NumTangentIntermediateBuffers

entries.help: Print break down of memory usage. 0: off (default), 1: print when out of memory, 2: print every frame
entries.name: r.SkinCache.PrintMemorySummary

entries.help: Use Transient memory for BLAS scratch allocation to reduce memory footprint and allocation overhead.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.RayTracingUseTransientForScratch

entries.help: This option enables recomputing the vertex tangents on the GPU.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.RecomputeTangents

entries.help: This option enables parallel dispatches for recompute tangents.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.RecomputeTangentsParallelDispatch

entries.help: Maximum memory allowed to be allocated per World/Scene in Megs
entries.name: r.SkinCache.SceneMemoryLimitInMB

entries.help: Reduce GPU Skin Vertex Factory shader permutations. Cannot be disabled while the skin cache is turned off.
entries.name: r.SkinCache.SkipCompilingGPUSkinVF

entries.help: Call BeginInitResource when the morph resources are finish streaming (DoFinishUpdate)
entries.name: r.Skinned.AllowSkinnedMorphDataStreaming

entries.help: Whether to prevent circular referencing when setting leader pose component or not.
entries.name: r.SkinnedMesh.PreventCircularReferencingOnSetLeaderPoseComponent

entries.help: Whether to flush the render thread (incurring a game thread stall) and clean existing LOD info when re-initializating.
entries.name: r.SkinnedMesh.ReleasePreviousLODInfoOnInitialization

entries.help: Update the streaming manager when the radius changes by more than this ratio since the last update. A negative value will disable the update.
entries.name: r.SkinnedMesh.UpdateBoundsNotifyStreamingRadiusChangeRatio

entries.help: Skips mesh draw call when the PSO is still compiling (default 0).
entries.name: r.SkipDrawOnPSOPrecaching

entries.help: If set will skip over creating DXT textures that are smaller than 4x4 or other invalid dimensions.
entries.name: r.SkipInvalidDXTDimensions

entries.help: Skip updates UpdatePrimitiveTransform is called redundantly, if the proxy allows it.
entries.name: r.SkipRedundantTransformUpdate

entries.help: SkyAtmosphere components are rendered when this is not 0, otherwise ignored.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere

entries.help: When enabled, a depth test will be used to not write pixel closer to the camera than StartDepth, effectively improving performance.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspective.DepthTest

entries.help: The length of the LUT in kilometers (default = 96km to get nice cloud/atmosphere interactions in the distance for default sky). Further than this distance, the last slice is used.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.Depth

entries.help: The number of depth slice to use for the aerial perspective volume texture.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.DepthResolution

entries.help: When enabled, the low resolution camera frustum/froxel volume containing atmospheric fog
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.FastApplyOnOpaque

entries.help: The sample count used per slice to evaluate aerial perspective. The effective sample count is usually lower and depends on SampleCountScale on the component as well as .ini files.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.SampleCountMaxPerSlice

entries.help: The distance in kilometer after which SampleCountMax samples will be used to ray march the atmosphere.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.DistanceToSampleCountMax

entries.help: Enable the generation the sky ambient lighting value.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.DistantSkyLightLUT

entries.help: The altitude at which the sky samples are taken to integrate the sky lighting. Default to 6km, typicaly cirrus clouds altitude.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.DistantSkyLightLUT.Altitude

entries.help: Enable the rendering of in editor notification to warn the user about missing sky dome pixels on screen. It is better to keep it enabled and will be removed when shipping.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.EditorNotifications

entries.help: When enabled, a look up texture is used to render the sky.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT

entries.help: Fast sky distance in kilometer after which at which SampleCountMax samples will be used to ray march the atmosphere.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT.DistanceToSampleCountMax

entries.help: Fast sky maximum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT.SampleCountMax

entries.help: Fast sky minimum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT.SampleCountMin

entries.help: Use full 32bit per-channel precision for all sky LUTs.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.LUT32

entries.help: The when enabled, 64 samples are used instead of 2, resulting in a more accurate multi scattering approximation (but also more expenssive).
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.MultiScatteringLUT.HighQuality

entries.help: The sample count used to evaluate multi-scattering.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.MultiScatteringLUT.SampleCount

entries.help: The maximum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance The effective sample count is usually lower and depends on distance and SampleCountScale on the component, as well as .ini files.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.SampleCountMax

entries.help: The minimum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.SampleCountMin

entries.help: Enable the sampling of atmospheric lights shadow map in order to produce volumetric shadows.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.SampleLightShadowmap

entries.help: Enable the generation of the sky transmittance.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.TransmittanceLUT

entries.help: The sample count used to evaluate transmittance.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.TransmittanceLUT.SampleCount

entries.help: If true, the transmittance LUT will use a small R8BG8B8A8 format to store data at lower quality.
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.TransmittanceLUT.UseSmallFormat

entries.help: SkyAtmosphere on async compute (default: false).
entries.name: r.SkyAtmosphereASyncCompute

entries.help: Make sure the sky light real time capture is not run on platform where it is considered out of budget. Cannot be changed at runtime.
entries.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture

entries.help: When enabled, the real-time sky light capture will have a depth buffer, this is for multiple meshes to be cover each other correctly. The height fog will also be applied according to the depth buffer.
entries.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.DepthBuffer

entries.help: Opaque meshes cast shadow from directional lights onto sky and clouds when enabled.
entries.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.ShadowFromOpaque

entries.help: When enabled, the real-time sky light capture and convolutions will by distributed over several frames to lower the per-frame cost. Value in [1,6].
entries.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.TimeSlice

entries.help: When enabled, the real-time sky light capture and convolutions will by distributed over several frames to lower the per-frame cost.
entries.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.TimeSlice.SkyCloudCubeFacePerFrame

entries.help: Defines the sky lighting quality which allows to adjust for performance.
entries.name: r.SkyLightingQuality

entries.help: Strength of skylight specular occlusion from DFAO (default is 1.0)
entries.name: r.SkySpecularOcclusionStrength

entries.help: Intensity scale on Stationary and Movable skylights.  This is useful to control overall lighting contrast in dynamically lit games with scalability levels which disable Ambient Occlusion.  For example, if medium quality disables SSAO and DFAO, reduce skylight intensity.

entries.help: Updates all stationary and movable skylights, useful for debugging the capture pipeline
entries.name: r.SkylightRecapture

entries.help: Whether to update all skylights every frame.  Useful for debugging.
entries.name: r.SkylightUpdateEveryFrame

entries.help: Mesh draw command caching is offloaded to an async task.
entries.name: r.SmartGI.AsyncCacheMeshDrawCommands

entries.help: Light primitive interactions are created off the render thread in an async task.
entries.name: r.SmartGI.AsyncCreateLightPrimitiveInteractions

entries.help: Card trace end distance from camera
entries.name: r.SmartGI.CardTraceEndDistanceFromCamera

entries.help: Cone trace method:
entries.name: r.SmartGI.ConeTraceMethod

entries.help: Performance tweak. Controls the granularity for the ParallelFor for clipmap culling.
entries.name: r.SmartGI.CullNumWordsPerTask

entries.help: Whether to gather lighting along the backface for the Two Sided Foliage shading model, which adds some GPU cost.  The final lighting is then DiffuseColor * FrontfaceLighting + SubsurfaceColor * BackfaceLighting.  When disabled, SubsurfaceColor will simply be added to DiffuseColor instead.

entries.help: Clamps the maximum ray lighting intensity (with PreExposure) to reduce fireflies.
entries.name: r.SmartGI.Reflections.MaxRayIntensity

entries.name: r.SmartGI.Reflections.MaxRoughnessToEvaluateRoughSpecular

entries.name: r.SmartGI.Reflections.Temporal

entries.help: Controls how TSR run on async compute. Some TSR passes can overlap with previous passes.
entries.name: r.TSR.AsyncCompute

entries.help: Experimental functionality to keep higher sharpness in TSR's history in motion.
entries.name: r.TSR.History.GrandReprojection

entries.help: Select the bitdepth of the history. r.TSR.History.R11G11B10=1 Saves memory bandwidth that is of particular interest of the TSR's UpdateHistory's runtime performance by saving memory both at previous frame's history reprojection and write out of the output and new history.
entries.name: r.TSR.History.R11G11B10

entries.help: Maximum number sample for each output pixel in the history. Higher values means more stability on highlights on static images, but may introduce additional ghosting on firefliers style of VFX. Minimum value supported is 8.0 as TSR was in 5.0 and 5.1. Maximum value possible due to the encoding of the TSR.History.Metadata is 32.0. Defaults to 16.0.
entries.name: r.TSR.History.SampleCount

entries.help: Resolution multiplier of the history of TSR based of output resolution. While increasing the resolution adds runtime cost to TSR, it allows to maintain a better sharpness and stability of the details stored in history through out the reprojection.
entries.name: r.TSR.History.ScreenPercentage

entries.help: Whether separate translucency should be accumulated separatly.
entries.name: r.TSR.History.SeparateTranslucency

entries.help: Selects shader permutation of the quality of the update of the history in the TSR HistoryUpdate pass currently driven by the sg.AntiAliasingQuality scalability group. For further details about what each offers, you are invited to look at DIM_UPDATE_QUALITY in TSRUpdateHistory.usf and customise to your need.
entries.name: r.TSR.History.UpdateQuality

entries.help: Controls the quality of TSR's built-in spatial anti-aliasing technology when the history needs to be rejected. While this may not be critical when the rendering resolution is not much lowered than display resolution, this technic however becomes essential to hide lower rendering resolution rendering because of two reasons:
entries.name: r.TSR.RejectionAntiAliasingQuality

entries.help: Instability in TSR output 99% of the time coming from instability of the shading rejection, for different reasons:
entries.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering

entries.help: Whether r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.Period settings should adjust to frame rate when below r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.FrameRateCap. Please read r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering's help for further details. (Enabled by default).
entries.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.AdjustToFrameRate

entries.help: Framerate cap in hertz at which point there is automatic adjustment of r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.Period when the rendering frame rate is lower. Please read r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering's help for further details. (Default to 60hz)
entries.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.FrameRateCap

entries.help: Some material might for instance might do something like parallax occlusion mapping such as CitySample's buildings' window's interiors. This often can not render accurately a motion vector of this fake interior geometry and therefore make the heuristic believe it is in fact flickering.
entries.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.MaxParallaxVelocity

entries.help: Periode in frames in which luma oscilations at equal or greater frequency is considered flickering and should ghost to stabilize the image Please read r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering's help for further details. (Default to 3 frames).
entries.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.Period

entries.help: Maximum number of sample in each output pixel of the history after total shading rejection.
entries.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.SampleCount

entries.help: Maximum age in frames of subpixel's depth kept in history for their self reprojection (default to 3 frames).
entries.name: r.TSR.Subpixel.DepthMaxAge

entries.help: Whether the depth of moving subpixel detail should also be included in the subpixel depth history for their reprojection. This is a really bad idea to turn this on because it is impossible how a moving object's velocity involves overtime when it's only occasionally drawing its velocity. (disabled by default).
entries.name: r.TSR.Subpixel.IncludeMovingDepth

entries.help: One particular challenge of Nanite amount of details is that sometimes these details can be thiner than a rendering pixel in which case they only render in some frames. When that happens, it means neither depth or velocity buffer to be able to reproject them. This is for instance visible with the `vis SceneDepthZ` command.
entries.name: r.TSR.Subpixel.Method

entries.help: Defines the output pixel velocity at which the the high frequencies of the history get's their contributing weight clamped. It's basically to lerp the effect of r.TSR.Velocity.WeightClampingSampleCount when the pixel velocity get smaller than r.TSR.Velocity.WeightClampingPixelSpeed. (Default = 1.0f).
entries.name: r.TSR.Velocity.WeightClampingPixelSpeed

entries.help: Number of sample to count to in history pixel to clamp history to when output pixel velocity reach r.TSR.Velocity.WeightClampingPixelSpeed. Higher value means higher stability on movement, but at the expense of additional blur due to successive convolution of each history reprojection.
entries.name: r.TSR.Velocity.WeightClampingSampleCount

entries.help: Whether to use wave ops in the shading rejection heuristics to speeds up convolutions.
entries.name: r.TSR.WaveOps
